channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: Google’s $40 Billion Bet on Anthropic’s Secret Mythos AI Model
item.description: Google’s recent $40 billion commitment to Anthropic marks a pivotal moment in the competitive AI landscape. This investment, which includes $10 billion upfront and $30 billion tied to performance milestones, grants Google access to Anthropic’s advanced AI mod… [Anthropic, Alphabet]

item.title: Could anything but profit steer artificial intelligence?
item.description: The trial pitting Elon Musk against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman made clear the two billionaires agreed on one thing: Building artificial intelligence would require significant resources — and enormous amounts of money. [Intel, OpenAI]

item.title: Could anything but profit steer AI? The OpenAI trial offered clues but no verdict
item.description: The trial pitting Elon Musk against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman made clear the two billionaires agreed on one thing: building artificial intelligence would require significant resources — and enormous amounts of money [Intel, OpenAI]

item.title: AI is killing the cheap smartphone. The memory that powers your phone now goes to data centres instead.
item.description: In 1985, the best computer a reasonably affluent American could buy was the IBM PC AT, which cost $19,400 in today’s money. Today, a Tecno Spark Go costs $30 in a Nairobi market stall and runs a processor billions of times faster. No other good in history has… [IBM, ServiceNow]

item.title: Warren Buffett sits on $400 bn cash as Michael Burry warns of AI bubble. Is a massive market crash coming?
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway holds nearly $400 billion in cash. Michael Burry warns of an AI bubble, citing similarities to the Dotcom crash. The AI boom is reshaping global markets. South Korea's Kospi and Taiwan's stock market have seen significant gains, surpassing …

item.title: Why AI And Why Now?
item.description: Opposition to AI and the enormous data centers it will need in the future is growing in the US, as people begin to see the risks. The post Why AI And Why Now? appeared first on CleanTechnica. [ServiceNow]

item.title: Navitas Semiconductor NVTS Stock Surges 20% on AI Power Demand and Upgraded Outlook
item.description: Navitas Semiconductor's shares rose nearly 20% due to investor interest in AI data center solutions, with Q1 2026 revenue up 18% and strategic focus on high-power markets.

item.title: Two Amazon data centres in the UAE were hit by drones. The Gulf’s AI ambitions are being tested.
item.description: Two Amazon Web Services data centres in the UAE were targeted early in the Middle East war. Nearly three months later, oil prices remain around $100 a barrel and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. The Gulf’s ambition to become a global AI hub is facing its … [Amazon]

item.title: India’s SolarSquare is raising $60M at a $500M valuation. It has powered 50,000 homes.
item.description: SolarSquare, an Indian rooftop solar startup, is in advanced talks to raise $55 million to $60 million at a valuation between $450 million and $500 million, TechCrunch reports. B Capital and Lightspeed Venture Partners are set to co-lead the Series C round. T…

item.title: Googling the Word “Disregard” Causes Google’s AI to Return Garbled Chatbot Ramblings
item.description: The future of Google Search, folks. The post Googling the Word “Disregard” Causes Google’s AI to Return Garbled Chatbot Ramblings appeared first on Futurism. [Alphabet]

item.title: As AI wipes out white-collar jobs, one Alabama high school and Toyota are training students for roles that pay $40 an hour and can’t be automated
item.description: The Huntsville Center for Technology gives high school students a foothold in the skilled trades amid a job market starved for that talent.
